title: reset_all drops core memory seed rows transactionally unsafe — TRIGGER fires during delete, FTS inconsistency on crash
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Functional Correctness (1) / Security and Access (5) perimeter

Location: src/memory.rs:1297-1314 (reset_all), combined with FTS triggers at lines 241-246.

Violation: reset_all runs one execute_batch containing DELETE FROM archival_memory; DELETE FROM core_memory; ... DELETE FROM file_relationships; ... INSERT INTO core_memory. The batch is NOT wrapped in a transaction. If the process dies after DELETE FROM archival_memory succeeds but before INSERT INTO core_memory, the database is left with no core_memory rows at all — the next session will start with no persona, no project_info, no user_preferences. The FTS5 triggers (archival_memory_ad, _au) fire during DELETE FROM archival_memory, deleting rows from archival_memory_fts one at a time via triggers — this is O(N) triggers per batch DELETE. For large archival tables this is SLOW and partial on crash.

Related issues elsewhere in the file:
- save_coding_pattern (908), save_error_pattern (1026), save_learned_preference (1120): all do SELECT then conditional UPDATE or INSERT without transactions. Two concurrent callers both see 'not found' and both INSERT the same row — the UNIQUE constraint doesn't exist on these tables, so duplicates accumulate silently with confidence counters diverging.
- cleanup_expired_short_term (850) does two separate DELETEs without a transaction — a crash between them leaves recent_sessions expired but recent_activity still there.
- memory_save (356) does INSERT then reads last_insert_rowid(); on a connection shared between threads (Mutex<Connection>), this is safe because the mutex is held, but the pattern breaks immediately if anyone ever replaces Mutex<Connection> with a connection pool.

Mandated Refactor:
1. Wrap reset_all in conn.transaction()?, COMMIT at end.
2. Same for all read-modify-write patterns (save_coding_pattern etc.).
3. Add UNIQUE constraints on the natural keys: coding_patterns(file_glob, pattern_type, description), learned_preferences(category, preference). Then use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE — atomic and race-free.
4. For reset_all, consider DROP+CREATE+re-seed in a single transaction as a nuke option, but keep the schema_version row.
5. Add a concurrency test: 10 threads racing save_coding_pattern with the same args, assert exactly ONE row exists with confidence=10.
